title: Poori with Aloo Bhaji

subs.name: half atta half maida for softer pooris

ingredients.name: water, just enough to form a stiff dough
potatoes, boiled, peeled and roughly mashed

subs.name: sweet potato boiled and mashed for a different twist

steps.text: Mix atta and salt. Add just enough water to form a firm, stiff dough — stiffer than roti dough. Knead for 5 minutes, cover and rest for 15 minutes. A stiff dough makes pooris that puff up properly.

Make the aloo bhaji: heat 2 tbsp oil in a kadai over medium flame. Add cumin seeds and let them splutter. Add sliced onion and cook until translucent, about 4 minutes. Add green chilli and ginger, stir for 1 minute.

Add turmeric and the roughly mashed potatoes. Stir well to combine. Add 100 ml water, season with salt, and cook on medium-low for 5 minutes, mashing a few more times so the bhaji is slightly chunky. Finish with coriander leaves and keep warm.
Heat oil for frying in a kadai to 180°C. Divide the poori dough into 16 small balls. Roll each into a 10 cm circle — do not make them too thin or they won't puff.

Carefully slide a poori into the hot oil. Use the back of a slotted spoon to gently press it down into the oil for 2–3 seconds — this creates steam pressure that puffs the poori. It should puff within about 20 seconds.

Flip the poori once and fry for another 30 seconds until both sides are a light golden colour. Drain on paper towels. Fry all the pooris, adjusting the heat to keep the oil at 180°C. Serve immediately alongside the warm aloo bhaji.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
safety_warnings: Keep water away from hot oil, lower food gently, avoid overfilling the vessel and never leave frying unattended.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: wheat-flour → half atta half maida for softer pooris; potato → sweet potato boiled and mashed for a different twist
